# Bayesian Classification: Traffic Accident Severity
# DS 4420 Final Project
# Predicts severity (1-4) from environmental/situational features

```{r}
library(brms)
library(readr)

# load cleaned data
accidents <- as.data.frame(read_csv("accidents_clean.csv"))

# feature engineering
# bin hour into time of day
accidents$TimeOfDay <- cut(accidents$Hour,
                           breaks = c(-1, 5, 11, 16, 20, 23),
                           labels = c("Night", "Morning", "Afternoon", "Evening", "LateNight"))

# simplify weather into broad buckets
accidents$Weather_Bucket <- ifelse(grepl("Rain|Drizzle|Shower",        accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Rain",
                            ifelse(grepl("Snow|Sleet|Hail|Ice|Wintry", accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Snow",
                            ifelse(grepl("Fog|Mist|Haze|Smoke",        accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Fog",
                            ifelse(grepl("Cloud|Overcast",              accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Cloudy",
                            ifelse(grepl("Clear|Fair|Sunny",            accidents$Weather_Condition, ignore.case=T), "Clear",
                            "Other")))))

# brms needs categorical variables as factors, not integers
accidents$IsNight <- as.factor(accidents$Sunrise_Sunset == "Night")
accidents$Severity <- as.factor(accidents$Severity)
accidents$Weather_Bucket <- as.factor(accidents$Weather_Bucket)
accidents$TimeOfDay <- as.factor(accidents$TimeOfDay)

# scale continuous features
accidents$Temp <- scale(accidents$`Temperature(F)`)
accidents$Humidity <- scale(accidents$`Humidity(%)`)
accidents$Visibility <- scale(accidents$`Visibility(mi)`)
accidents$Distance <- scale(accidents$`Distance(mi)`)

# downsample to balance classes (severity 2 and 3 dominate)
set.seed(42)
target_n <- 5000
balanced <- do.call(rbind, lapply(1:4, function(k) {
  s <- accidents[accidents$Severity == k, ]
  s[sample(nrow(s), min(nrow(s), target_n)), ]
}))

# train/test split (80/20)
train_idx <- sample(nrow(balanced), floor(0.8 * nrow(balanced)))
train <- balanced[train_idx, ]
test  <- balanced[-train_idx, ]

print(table(train$Severity))
```

```{r}
# check default priors
default_prior(Severity ~ Temp + Humidity + Visibility + Distance + IsNight + Weather_Bucket + TimeOfDay,
              data = train,
              family = categorical("logit"))

# define manual priors
manual_prior <- c(
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu2"),
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu3"),
  brms::prior(student_t(3, 0, 5), class = "Intercept", dpar = "mu4"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu2"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu3"),
  brms::prior(normal(0, 1), class = "b", dpar = "mu4")
)

# fit the model
severity_brm <- brm(
  Severity ~ Temp + Humidity + Visibility + Distance + IsNight + Weather_Bucket + TimeOfDay,
  family  = categorical("logit"),
  data    = train,
  chains  = 4,
  iter    = 2000,
  warmup  = 500,
  thin    = 2,
  cores   = getOption("mc.cores", 4),
  prior   = manual_prior
)

# diagnostics
summary(severity_brm)
plot(severity_brm)

# conditional effects
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Temp",       categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Visibility", categorical=TRUE))
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "IsNight",    categorical=TRUE))

# posterior predictive for one accident
# each row is an MCMC sample, each column a test observation
# values are predicted severity class labels
post_preds <- posterior_predict(severity_brm, newdata=test)

# accuracy on test set
pred_classes <- apply(post_preds, 2, function(x) as.integer(names(which.max(table(x)))))
cat("Accuracy:", round(mean(pred_classes == as.integer(test$Severity)), 4), "\n")

# confusion matrix
print(table(Predicted=pred_classes, Actual=as.integer(test$Severity)))

# posterior predictive distribution for one observation
hist(post_preds[, 1], main="Post Pred Distribution: Accident 1",
     xlab="Predicted Severity", col="steelblue", border="white")
cat("True severity:", as.integer(test$Severity[1]), "\n")

# credible interval plot
plot(conditional_effects(severity_brm, "Humidity", categorical=TRUE))
```
